title: Execution contract exists before implementation code
summary: No feature implementation may be declared implementation-entry ready without an execution-contract entry derived from the active governed corpus.

title: The first code sprint remains intentionally bounded
affects_requirements: UDQ-REQ-ARCH-001; UDQ-REQ-ARCH-002; UDQ-REQ-AUD-001; UDQ-REQ-AUD-002; UDQ-REQ-AUD-003; UDQ-REQ-EVT-001; UDQ-REQ-EVT-002; UDQ-REQ-GOV-001; UDQ-REQ-GOV-002; UDQ-REQ-GOV-003; UDQ-REQ-GOV-004; UDQ-REQ-HIS-001; UDQ-REQ-HIS-002; UDQ-REQ-IMPL-001; UDQ-REQ-OUT-001; UDQ-REQ-OUT-002; UDQ-REQ-PROF-001; UDQ-REQ-PROF-002; UDQ-REQ-REL-001; UDQ-REQ-SIG-001; UDQ-REQ-SIG-002; UDQ-REQ-UI-001; UDQ-REQ-UI-003; UDQ-REQ-UI-004; UDQ-REQ-UI-006
summary: The first code sprint is constrained to the structural and conformance-aware behavioral spine documented in docs/handbook/IMPLEMENTATION_ENTRY.md.
affected_requirement_ids: UDQ-REQ-ARCH-001; UDQ-REQ-ARCH-002; UDQ-REQ-AUD-001; UDQ-REQ-AUD-002; UDQ-REQ-AUD-003; UDQ-REQ-EVT-001; UDQ-REQ-EVT-002; UDQ-REQ-GOV-001; UDQ-REQ-GOV-002; UDQ-REQ-GOV-003; UDQ-REQ-GOV-004; UDQ-REQ-HIS-001; UDQ-REQ-HIS-002; UDQ-REQ-IMPL-001; UDQ-REQ-OUT-001; UDQ-REQ-OUT-002; UDQ-REQ-PROF-001; UDQ-REQ-PROF-002; UDQ-REQ-REL-001; UDQ-REQ-SIG-001; UDQ-REQ-SIG-002; UDQ-REQ-UI-001; UDQ-REQ-UI-003; UDQ-REQ-UI-004; UDQ-REQ-UI-006

title: Outputs command-state semantics are allowed, but physical actuation is not
summary: The first code sprint may implement command-state structure, mismatch, and evidence scaffolding, but not physical output writes.

title: Remote command actuation remains blocked
summary: Remote command parity remains outside the first code sprint until local proof/evidence and authorization scaffolding exist in running code.

title: Rules and sequences remain deferred beyond the first sprint
summary: These areas remain semantically closed but are intentionally postponed until after the first code sprint establishes the baseline behavioral spine.

title: Human-facing control-tower docs remain required
summary: Every package revision shall continue to include root-level status, entry, and next-action docs so a human can catch up quickly.

title: First code sprint must create traceability and proof scaffolding
summary: The first code sprint is not allowed to be feature-only; it must attach to requirement IDs, invariant hooks, and proof-bundle scaffolding.

title: Implementation-facing UI package uses ui naming
summary: Use src/universaldaq/ui as the implementation-facing package name while retaining frontend/backend authority language at the architectural level where useful.

title: Typed domain model sprint precedes adapter and widget-heavy work
summary: The next sprint will implement pure typed models and convert placeholder behavior tests before adapter, remote, or heavy widget work begins.

title: Reserved packages remain guarded until scope expands
summary: Deferred packages remain present as reserved directories and must not gain active implementation files during the next sprint.

title: One local gate and one CI gate are canonical
summary: The preferred repo validation flow is the stable local gate mirrored by CI running in strict mode.

title: Sprint 1 changes must open with a documentation impact map
summary: Any bounded Sprint 1 change must declare its documentation impact, governing sources, and reconciliation outputs before it can be considered complete.
